title: Quiz: Quantum Regression
description: Test your understanding of quantum regression methods

questions.question: How does a variational quantum regressor produce continuous output values?

options.text: By counting the number of 1s in measurement outcomes
By computing the expectation value of an observable
By measuring the circuit execution time
By counting the total number of gates

questions.explanation: Quantum regressors use expectation values of observables (typically Pauli Z) to produce continuous outputs in the range [-1, 1].

questions.question: What is the typical output range of a quantum regressor using Z expectation values?
questions.explanation: The expectation value of Pauli Z ranges from -1 (all |1⟩) to +1 (all |0⟩), so targets should be scaled accordingly.

questions.question: What loss function is most commonly used for quantum regression?
questions.explanation: Mean squared error is the standard loss for regression tasks, measuring the average squared difference between predictions and targets.

questions.question: Quantum kernel regression requires optimizing variational parameters in the quantum circuit.
questions.explanation: Quantum kernel regression uses a fixed feature map. The optimization happens in the classical kernel ridge regression, not in the quantum circuit.

questions.question: In multi-output quantum regression, how are multiple outputs typically obtained?

options.text: Running the circuit multiple times with different inputs
Measuring expectation values on different qubits

questions.explanation: Multi-output regression measures expectation values of different qubits or different observables to produce multiple continuous outputs.

questions.question: Why is bounded encoding (like tanh) often used in quantum regression?

options.text: To prevent unbounded rotation angles causing instabilities
To reduce the number of qubits needed

questions.explanation: Bounded encodings like tanh map inputs to a finite range, preventing extreme rotation angles that can cause gradient instabilities during training.

questions.question: What does an R² score of 0.95 indicate in regression?

options.text: 95% of variance in targets is explained by the model
The model uses 95% of available qubits

questions.explanation: R² (coefficient of determination) measures the proportion of variance in the target variable explained by the model. R²=0.95 means 95% of variance is captured.

questions.question: Which are valid approaches for quantum regression? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Variational quantum regressors using expectation values
Measuring qubit phases directly for output

questions.explanation: Variational regressors (a), kernel methods (b), and Gaussian processes with quantum kernels (c) are valid approaches. Direct phase measurement (d) is not a standard regression technique.
